title: What Workfusion should help EA builders fix first
summary: The highest-intent EA problems are compiler errors, invalid stops, no-trade backtests, and unclear risk controls.
body: Workfusion growth autopilot is publishing only to owned Workfusion channels by default. Third-party forums and social posts stay in a manual-review outbox unless official API posting is configured.
The product focus remains narrow: generate EA drafts, debug MQL compiler errors, score readiness, organize projects, and download MQL outputs.
The best first users are MQL developers, EA builders, prop-style traders who need risk checks, and educators who teach MetaTrader automation.
Workfusion does not trade for users, does not request broker credentials, and does not promise profitable backtests.

title: EA debugging patterns from today's public questions
summary: Invalid stops, MT5 history functions, and magic-number reporting are the clearest Workfusion use cases this week.
body: Today's public EA questions show the same pattern: most builders are not blocked by strategy first. They are blocked by development workflow issues.
The recurring issues are invalid stops on XAUUSD, MT5 history/deal functions causing compiler errors, and the need to evaluate multiple EAs by magic number from account history.
Workfusionapp is built for this workflow: generate a complete EA draft, debug MQL compiler errors, risk-check the code, organize projects, and download the MQL output before manual testing.
This is a development tool, not a signal service or trading-performance promise.
